blocks.text: DTC P1765: Accumulator Control SOL.V. Circuit Disconnected

V. opens and closes the accumulator circuit by turning it on and off, and supplies the hydraulic pressure stored in the accumulator control SOL.V. to the main circuit when starting from an idle stop. The accumulator control SOL.V. is driven by a drive circuit under command from the PGM-FI ECU. This drive circuit has a diagnostic function that detects and outputs a load disconnection or short circuit status, and returns a return signal to the command. If the return signal logic is unjustified for the OFF command from the PGM-FI ECU to the accumulator control SOL.V., the PGM-FI ECU judges a failure and stores the DTC.
Detection frequency, detection sequence, detection time required, detection method type, OBD status
Accumulator If the return signal logic continues in an unjustified state for 1 second or longer in response to the OFF command of the control SOL.V.

items.text: Disconnection of cord (SOLIS line) between PGM-FI ECU and accumulator control SOL. (including disconnected or loose couplers)
Accumulator Control SOL.V. Internal circuit disconnection

blocks.text: Start the engine and hold the select lever in the P position for at least 10 seconds.
If the vehicle is determined to be faulty, the D indicator light flashes without the PGM-FI warning light illuminating, and the temporary DTC, DTC and freeze data are stored in the PGM-FI ECU memory.
Use of the clear command of the scan tool (including HDS) or removal of the 12V battery terminal will clear the D indicator light, temporary DTC, DTC, and freeze data.
